engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi L 400 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi L 400 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The backbone of the L 400 range - reliable indirect injection diesel with strong commercial durability and widespread UK aftermarket support.
compatibleVariants: GL, GLX, Van, Wagon, 2WD, 4WD, High Roof, Long Wheelbase, Camper Conversion, Delica Space Gear
commonFailures: Timing belt failure at 60k miles causing catastrophic valve damage
Injector pump wear leading to hard starting and power loss
Turbocharger oil seal leaks on high-mileage 4D56T units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D56 Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: Turbocharged variant delivering higher torque for 4WD and camper conversions - popular with off-road enthusiasts and commercial operators.
compatibleVariants: Wagon, Super Exceed, 4WD, Delica Space Gear, Camper Conversion
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure above 120k miles
Intercooler piping cracks causing boost loss
EGR valve clogging on high-mileage commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D56T Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Larger displacement diesel introduced for Super Exceed and heavy-duty applications - higher torque output for towing and off-road use.
compatibleVariants: Super Exceed, Long Wheelbase, 4WD, Camper Conversion
commonFailures: Injector pump calibration drift causing rough running
Cylinder head gasket failure on overheated units
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: Premium turbo variant for maximum torque - favoured by serious off-roaders and commercial users requiring heavy-load capability.
compatibleVariants: Super Exceed 4WD, Long Wheelbase 4WD, Heavy-Duty Camper
commonFailures: Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage units
Fuel injector nozzle wear causing smoke and power loss
Oil cooler gasket leaks leading to coolant contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M40T Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: SOHC petrol option for early L 400 models - less common in UK but valued for simplicity and lower emissions in urban use.
compatibleVariants: GL Petrol, GLX Petrol, Wagon Petrol, 2WD variants
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing
Timing belt tensioner failure causing valve interference
Distributor cap and rotor wear leading to misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level petrol engine for base trim L 400 - rare in UK market but occasionally sought for lightweight camper conversions.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption
Water pump failure leading to overheating
Ignition coil degradation on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G63 Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Rare V6 option for export markets - very limited UK availability but occasionally imported for specialist camper or performance conversions.
compatibleVariants: Imported Super Exceed, Specialist Camper Conversions
commonFailures: Timing belt service neglect causing interference damage
Oil pump drive wear on high-mileage V6 units
Intake manifold gasket leaks causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G72 Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi L 400 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi L 400's 4D56 and 4M40 diesel engines dominate UK replacement demand, with timing belt failures and injector issues most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific L 400 variant.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 Timing Belt Failure - Why Preventative Replacement Matters
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L 400 Van, Wagon, GL, GLX, Super Exceed (1994-2004, 4D56 & 4D56T)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 55,000-75,000 miles if not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, pistons strike valves causing catastrophic engine damage requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Belt Kit Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.tier: Major - Cylinder Head Repair Post Belt Failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve replacement, head machining, gaskets, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block undamaged; often not cost-effective vs replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine, upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for business-critical vehicles; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi L 400 worth £3,000-£8,000, preventative timing belt replacement at 60k miles is far cheaper than post-failure repairs. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt 4D56 engine from our network offers better long-term value than head repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4D56 engine prices for your Mitsubishi L 400

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 Turbo Failure - Symptoms & Replacement Options
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L 400 Wagon, Super Exceed, 4WD, Delica Space Gear (1996-2004, 4D56T)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear from oil starvation or coked oil passages; failed seals allow oil into intake/exhaust causing blue smoke and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, refurbish or exchange turbo, new gaskets, oil feed line check
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; addresses smoke and boost loss

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4D56T Engine with New Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine including new/reconditioned turbo, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vehicles; eliminates multiple failure risks

problems.recommendation: If your 4D56T shows early turbo warning signs (whine, smoke, boost loss) but the engine is otherwise sound, a turbo exchange is cost-effective. For engines over 120k miles with additional wear signs, a full rebuilt unit offers better long-term reliability for business use.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4D56T turbo diesel Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 Injector Pump Failure - Diagnosis & Solutions
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L 400 Van, Wagon, GL, GLX, Super Exceed (1994-2004, 4D56 & 4D56T)
problems.rootCause: Mechanical injection pump internal components wear, causing poor calibration, hard starting, rough running and power loss; contamination accelerates wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, bench test, recalibrate or exchange unit, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if injectors and engine otherwise healthy; restores performance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4D56 with New Injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine including calibrated injectors, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vehicles with multiple wear issues

problems.recommendation: For L 400s under 150k miles with isolated pump issues, pump recalibration offers good value. For vehicles with additional wear signs or critical commercial use, a rebuilt engine with calibrated injectors included provides better long-term reliability and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare 4D56 diesel engine prices with injector calibration for your Mitsubishi L 400

problems.group: Mitsubishi L 400 4M40 Cylinder Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Mitsubishi L 400 4M40 Cylinder Head Gasket Failure - When to Replace
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L 400 Super Exceed, Long Wheelbase, 4WD (1997-2004, 4M40 & 4M40T)
problems.rootCause: Head gasket fails due to thermal stress or coolant loss; allows coolant into cylinders or oil passages, causing overheating, white smoke, or oil contamination.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Cylinder Head Recondition & Gasket Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, new gasket, valves checked, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block undamaged and no warping; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with reconditioned head, upgraded components, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial 4M40s; eliminates recurrence and other wear risks

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi L 400 Super Exceed or commercial 4M40 over 130k miles, head gasket repair is often a short-term fix. A rebuilt 4M40 engine offers better value for business-critical vehicles, with warranty protection against related failures.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 rebuilt engine for your Mitsubishi L 400

problems.group: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear
problems.h4: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L 400 Van, Wagon, GL, GLX, 4WD (1994-2004, high-mileage 4D56)
problems.rootCause: Piston rings and cylinder bores wear from extended oil change intervals or poor maintenance; causes blue smoke, high oil consumption and loss of compression.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace pistons/rings, hone bores, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if cylinder head and ancillaries healthy; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new pistons/rings, upgraded components, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vehicles; eliminates multiple wear points with warranty

problems.recommendation: For L 400s with oil consumption but otherwise healthy, a short block overhaul can be cost-effective. For vehicles with additional wear signs or critical commercial use, a full rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4D56 engine prices for high-mileage Mitsubishi L 400

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi L 400
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing belt damage, turbo failure, injector issues or head gasket problems on your L 400, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi L 400 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your commercial vehicle or camper back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi L 400 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi L 400 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi L 400 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre 4D56 diesel is the most common L 400 engine, powering Van, Wagon and GL variants from 1994-2004 with indirect injection reliability and strong aftermarket support across the UK commercial sector.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi L 400 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The larger 2.8-litre 4M40 diesel was introduced for Super Exceed and heavy-duty L 400 variants from 1997, offering higher torque for towing, off-road use and camper conversions requiring extra pulling power.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi L 400 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 2.0-litre 4G63 petrol powered early base-trim L 400 models, offering simplicity and lower emissions for urban use but rare in the UK market compared to diesel variants.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4-litre 4G64 SOHC petrol was the main petrol option for L 400 GL and GLX variants, valued for smooth operation and lower emissions but less common than diesel in UK commercial and camper applications.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The rare 3.0-litre 6G72 V6 petrol was occasionally fitted to imported Super Exceed or specialist camper L 400s, offering smooth power but very limited UK availability and specialist sourcing requirements.

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate the UK L 400 market, with the 4D56 2.5L and 4M40 2.8L powering most Van, Wagon and Super Exceed variants from 1994-2004.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate the UK L 400 market, with the 4D56 2.5L and 4M40 2.8L powering most Van, Wagon and Super Exceed variants from 1994-2004.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi L 400 Diesel Engine Families
families: 4D56 - most common replacement engine (Van/Wagon/GL), timing belt critical at 60k miles
4D56T - turbo variant for 4WD/camper use, watch for turbo oil seal leaks
4M40 - larger 2.8L for Super Exceed/heavy duty, injector pump calibration key
4M40T - turbo 4M40 for maximum torque, turbo actuator maintenance essential
knownFor: Strong commercial durability with proper timing belt maintenance
4D56 timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage if neglected
4M40 offers higher torque for towing and off-road camper conversions
High UK replacement demand on commercial Van and Wagon variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi L 400 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mitsubishi L 400 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol L 400s are less common in the UK but the 4G64 2.4L and rare 6G72 V6 offer smooth operation for urban or specialist camper applications.
items.descriptor: Petrol L 400s are less common in the UK but the 4G64 2.4L and rare 6G72 V6 offer smooth operation for urban or specialist camper applications.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi L 400 Petrol Engine Families
families: 4G64 - main petrol option (GL/GLX), watch for oil sludge from infrequent servicing
4G63 - entry-level 2.0L, rare in UK, valve stem seals common wear item
6G72 - rare 3.0L V6, imported variants only, timing belt critical
knownFor: Simpler maintenance than diesel but higher fuel consumption
Oil change intervals critical to prevent sludge buildup on 4G64
Timing belt replacement essential on all petrol variants to prevent interference damage
Lower UK replacement demand but specialist sourcing often required
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi L 400 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi L 400's UK production run (1994-2004), helping you identify your engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The L 400 launched with the proven 4D56 diesel and 4G64 petrol, establishing the platform for UK commercial and passenger use.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi L 400 introduced to UK market replacing earlier Delica Star Wagon
4D56 2.5L indirect injection diesel becomes the volume seller
4G64 2.4L SOHC petrol offered for base GL/GLX variants
2WD and early 4WD variants available from launch
knownFor: Robust 4D56 design with strong commercial durability
Timing belt replacement critical at 60k miles to prevent valve damage
Simple mechanical injection easier to maintain than later common-rail systems
Early 4WD systems require compatibility verification during engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1996 Mitsubishi L 400 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 timing belt replacement • Mitsubishi L 400 GL engine replacement cost • Mitsubishi L 400 Van diesel engine supply
enquiries: Mitsubishi L 400 GL engine replacement cost
Mitsubishi L 400 Van diesel engine supply

years.description: The premium Super Exceed trim launched with the larger 4M40 2.8L diesel, expanding the L 400's capability for towing and off-road use.
keyChanges: Super Exceed premium trim introduced with enhanced interior and equipment
4M40 2.8L turbo diesel added for higher torque applications
4D56T turbo variant became standard on 4WD Wagon and Delica Space Gear
Emissions refinements for evolving UK regulations
knownFor: 4M40 offers significantly higher torque for towing and camper conversions
Turbo variants (4D56T/4M40T) require careful oil maintenance to protect bearings
Super Exceed trim commands higher resale and replacement engine investment
4WD compatibility verification essential when sourcing replacement engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-1999 Mitsubishi L 400 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi L 400 Super Exceed engine replacement • Mitsubishi L 400 4M40 turbo failure • Delica Space Gear 4D56T engine cost
enquiries: Mitsubishi L 400 Super Exceed engine replacement

years.description: The facelifted L 400 received updated electronics and emissions systems while retaining the proven 4D56 and 4M40 diesel engines through end of UK production.
keyChanges: Front-end facelift with revised grille and lighting
Updated engine management electronics for emissions compliance
Final UK production year 2004, limited availability thereafter
Camper conversion market grows using L 400 chassis and engines
knownFor: Final production units often well-maintained by commercial fleets
Timing belt and injector pump remain key maintenance items on high-mileage units
Camper conversion weight/load considerations affect engine selection
Specialist sourcing required for post-2004 replacement engines due to limited UK supply
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2004 Mitsubishi L 400 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi L 400 camper engine replacement • Mitsubishi L 400 2004 engine availability • Mitsubishi L 400 4M40 injector pump replacement

faq.h2: Mitsubishi L 400 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi L 400 engine typically costs £950-£2,400 for the common 4D56 diesel, with 4M40 units ranging £1,200-£2,800. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on location and variant.
keyPoints: 4D56 rebuilt engines from £950 supply-only across UK network
4M40 Super Exceed engines command premium pricing due to torque output
Supply & fit service available nationwide through vetted commercial specialists
Prices include upgraded components addressing common 4D56/4M40 failure points
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Mitsubishi L 400?
items.answer: The primary engine codes for UK Mitsubishi L 400 models are 4D56 (2.5L diesel), 4D56T (turbo), 4M40 (2.8L diesel) and 4G64 (2.4L petrol), with variant-specific compatibility.
keyPoints: 4D56/4D56T power most Van, Wagon, GL and GLX variants 1994-2004
4M40/4M40T fitted to Super Exceed and heavy-duty applications 1997-2004
4G64 petrol rare in UK but available for early GL/GLX models
Use our free engine code checker with your registration for instant compatibility
items.cta: Check your Mitsubishi L 400 engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi L 400 4D56 engine reliable after rebuild?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt 4D56 engines from our UK network include upgraded timing components and injector calibration, with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty for commercial peace of mind.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units replace timing belt, tensioner and wear items proactively
Injector pump calibration included to prevent rough running and power loss
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across specialist network
Ideal for commercial Van/Wagon operators needing reliable, warrantied power
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4D56 Mitsubishi L 400 engines with warranty

items.question: Can I fit a 4M40 engine in my Mitsubishi L 400 originally fitted with 4D56?
items.answer: Engine swaps between 4D56 and 4M40 are possible but require ECU, transmission and 4WD system compatibility checks - consult a Mitsubishi commercial specialist before proceeding.
keyPoints: 4M40 is physically larger and produces higher torque than 4D56
Transmission bell housing, mounts and 4WD transfer case must match
ECU and wiring harness modifications often required for compatibility
Professional assessment recommended to avoid costly fitment issues
items.cta: Speak to a Mitsubishi L 400 engine specialist about compatibility

items.question: How long does Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 working days for supply-only rebuilt engines, with supply & fit appointments scheduled within 7-10 days depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines dispatched within 48 hours of order confirmation
4WD and camper variants may require additional compatibility verification
Supply & fit appointments coordinated through UK commercial specialist network
Nationwide delivery available on all engine types with tracking
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi L 400 engine availability and delivery times

items.question: What warranty comes with Mitsubishi L 400 replacement engines?
items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Mitsubishi L 400 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK network specialist location
Extended warranty options available for commercial fleet applications
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L 400 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I know if my Mitsubishi L 400 needs engine replacement?
items.answer: Key signs include persistent knocking, excessive blue/white smoke, loss of power despite servicing, or catastrophic timing belt failure - get a free diagnostic consultation to confirm.
keyPoints: Knocking noises often indicate bearing wear requiring short block rebuild
Blue smoke suggests turbo oil seal or piston ring wear on high-mileage units
White smoke and coolant loss may signal head gasket failure on 4M40
Timing belt failure causes immediate valve damage requiring full replacement
items.cta: Get a free Mitsubishi L 400 engine diagnostic consultation

items.question: Are Mitsubishi L 400 4WD engines different from 2WD?
items.answer: Yes, 4WD Mitsubishi L 400 engines require compatibility verification with the transfer case, mounting points and drivetrain - our specialists confirm 4WD fitment before dispatch.
keyPoints: 4D56T and 4M40T turbo variants commonly fitted to 4WD models
Transfer case mounting points and engine mounts differ from 2WD
4WD compatibility verification included with every quote request
Essential for off-road, camper and commercial 4x4 applications
items.cta: Verify 4WD compatibility for your Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Mitsubishi L 400 engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, supply & fit is available nationwide through our network of vetted Mitsubishi commercial specialists, with typical added cost of £450-£850 depending on location and variant.
keyPoints: Supply & fit includes removal, fitting, fluids and initial run-in
4WD and camper variants receive compatibility verification during fitment
Nationwide network minimises vehicle downtime for commercial operators
Book appointments directly through your chosen specialist after quote comparison

items.question: What's the difference between used, rebuilt and reconditioned Mitsubishi L 400 engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with basic warranty; rebuilt engines replace all wear items with upgraded components; reconditioned units undergo near-new restoration with full testing.
keyPoints: Used: budget option, request compression test and service history
Rebuilt: best value for commercial use, upgraded timing/injector components
Reconditioned: near-new standard, ideal for premium Super Exceed variants
All options include minimum 12-month warranty through EngineMarket network
